title: Place memory NUMA-aware
description: Allocate and touch memory on the node where the thread that uses it runs, so hot data avoids slow cross-socket access.
body: Use when running on a multi-socket machine and profiling shows remote-memory latency or bandwidth limits. Do not allocate all memory on one node then spread threads across sockets. Actions: pin threads to cores, rely on first-touch by initializing data on the consuming thread, or use explicit NUMA allocation, and verify with numastat that remote accesses drop. Validation signals include lower remote-node memory traffic and improved scaling across sockets on representative load. Risk boundary: aggressive pinning can fight the OS scheduler and hurt other tenants; on shared hosts, confirm the affinity policy with the operator before enforcing it.
